action.thought_summary: Starting with interaction behavior - need to understand what editing operations users need, as this is core to the product.
action.question: What specific image editing operations should users be able to perform (e.g., cropping, resizing, filters, color adjustments, text overlay)?

action.thought_summary: User has specified basic editing operations. Need to probe interaction behavior next per coverage schedule. Key interaction aspects: undo/redo, applying multiple edits sequentially, previewing changes before applying.
action.question: Should users be able to undo/redo their edits, and can they apply multiple editing operations to the same image sequentially (e.g., crop first, then resize, then add watermark)?

action.thought_summary: User mentioned 'manage their images' in initial requirement but we've only covered editing operations. Need to probe image management aspects like uploading, saving, organizing. Also need to make questions more concrete since user found previous question too broad.
action.question: When users finish editing an image, what should happen next — should they be able to download the edited image to their device, save it to an online gallery within the site, or both?

action.thought_summary: The user mentioned 'manage their images' in the initial requirement, but we've only covered editing operations and downloading. Need to probe what image management means - could include uploading, organizing, deleting, or cataloging images.
action.question: You mentioned managing images as well as editing them — should users be able to upload and store multiple images on the site, organize them into folders or albums, or is the site purely for editing one image at a time with no persistent storage?

action.thought_summary: User wants login and secure image management but is unsure about folders/albums. Need to probe specifically about how they envision users viewing and interacting with their uploaded images - a simple list, thumbnails, recent edits, etc. This falls under content/data category which should be covered by turn 5.
action.question: When a logged-in user views their uploaded images, should they see a simple list showing all their images with thumbnails, or would it be helpful to have features like marking favorites, seeing recently edited images, or filtering by upload date?

action.thought_summary: User has been uncertain about organizational features. I should ask a concrete, specific question about a practical aspect of image management they're likely to have an opinion on, such as deletion or storage limits, rather than broad display/organization questions.
action.question: Should users be able to delete images they've uploaded, and should there be a limit on how many images a user can store in their account (e.g., maximum 50 images or 500MB total)?

action.thought_summary: User has been uncertain about broad management questions. Pivoting to a more specific content/data question about watermark functionality, which was mentioned but not explored. This could reveal implicit requirements about customization options.
action.question: When users add a watermark, should they be able to customize it — for example, choose between text or an image as the watermark, adjust its opacity, or pick its position on the image (like bottom-right corner or tiled across the whole image)?

action.thought_summary: User has been uncertain about many specifics. Need to ask more concrete, decision-facilitating questions. Moving toward style/UI category since we're past turn 6. Will ask about output format since it's a concrete follow-up to the download feature they confirmed.
action.question: When users download their edited image, should they be able to choose the output format (e.g., JPEG, PNG, WebP) and quality level, or should the site always export in the same format as the original upload?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many feature specifics. I should try a more concrete, scenario-based question about the editing workflow to help them articulate what they need. This covers interaction behavior.
action.question: When a user is editing an image — say they've just cropped it and want to resize it next — should they see a live preview of each change as they make adjustments (like dragging a slider and seeing the result instantly), or should they apply each edit and then see the result?

action.thought_summary: User has been uncertain about many detailed feature questions. I should try a more concrete, scenario-based question. I've covered interaction and content categories; should probe style/UI. A practical question about device support or visual style direction might get a clearer answer.
action.question: Should this website work on mobile devices (phones/tablets) as well as desktop computers, or is it primarily designed for desktop use?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many specifics. I should try a more concrete, scenario-based question to help them think about actual needs. I'll ask about the primary use case or who the users are, which can reveal implicit requirements about features like batch processing, image types, or workflow patterns.
action.question: Who are the primary users of this website — for example, are they photographers watermarking their portfolio, e-commerce sellers processing product images, or casual users doing quick photo edits? This will help me understand what features matter most.

action.thought_summary: User has been uncertain on many specifics. Middleware suggests covering style/UI. I should ask a concrete, easy-to-answer question about visual preferences rather than broad open-ended ones.
action.question: Should the website have a dark theme (dark background with light text) or a light theme (white background with dark text) for the editing interface?

action.thought_summary: User just revealed specific color preferences (lavender blush background, pale violet red components). This is a style/UI finding. I should probe further on concrete UI/layout preferences while the user is engaged on this topic, as middleware suggests focusing on visual style.
action.question: When a user is editing an image, how should the editing workspace be laid out — should the editing tools (crop, rotate, resize, watermark) appear as a sidebar on the left with the image preview on the right, or should the tools be in a toolbar across the top with the image below it?

action.thought_summary: The user has provided color preferences (lavender blush background, pale violet red components) but been uncertain about layout. I should ask a more specific, concrete UI/style question that builds on the colors they already chose, such as button styling or typography.
action.question: You mentioned pale violet red for components — should the buttons on the site have rounded corners with a soft, friendly look, or sharp square corners for a more modern, crisp feel?

action.thought_summary: The user has specified lavender blush background and pale violet red components. Many detailed questions have received uncertain responses. I should try one more concrete style question about typography or spacing before considering finishing, as the middleware emphasizes style/UI coverage.
action.question: Should the text throughout the website use a clean, modern sans-serif font (like Arial or Roboto) or a more decorative serif font (like Georgia or Times New Roman)?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many specific details across interaction, content, and style categories. Key confirmed requirements include: editing features (crop, rotate, resize, watermark), user login/accounts, image upload, local download, and a specific color theme (lavender blush background, pale violet red components). With the turn budget nearly exhausted and the user consistently unsure about further specifics, it's time to finish and summarize what was elicited.
action.finish_summary: Elicited requirements for the image processing website: 1) Editing features: cropping, rotating, resizing, and adding watermarks to images. 2) User accounts: users must log in to access their account and manage images securely. 3) Image upload: users can upload their own images for processing. 4) Download: users can download processed/edited images to save locally. 5) Visual theme: lavender blush background color with pale violet red components, creating a light and colorful theme. Many detailed preferences (undo/redo, sequential editing workflow, watermark customization, output format options, live preview behavior, image organization/folders, storage limits, mobile compatibility, workspace layout, button styling, typography) were probed but the user did not express strong preferences, leaving them as flexible or to-be-decided design choices.
